subject: OOF Integration Changes
commit_message:
OOF Integration Changes

Thank you for the very detailed Review Boris.
All changes you had asked have been incorported.

Please take a look and let me know.
Few important points.

All components excep API and Music are services
that do not expose any TCP or HTTP ports. All the NodePort
and serices section areas in each of these component's
values.yaml file have been cleaned. Actually, the service
section do not make sense of these components at all.
They use Music as a distributed Q to receive and push their
tasks/changes.

These include a). Controller b). Data c). Solver and 4). Reservation.

This is why you didn't see any service.yaml files for these.

-------------------------------

Made a single configuration based setup.
All HAS components are now configured through
a single configmap and a single set of configuration
files.

All oof-has components share configurations.
Restructured the charts so that theere is
one parent for oof-has from where properties
can be inherited.

Removed other issues like the make was failing.

All PODs are coming up except data - which depends
on AAI certs - team is looking for that side.

Issue-ID: OPTFRA-22
Change-Id: I4bf36a68fc3636c822f33f55da70ace577c64ac0
Signed-off-by: Ikram Ikramullah <ikram@research.att.com>
Signed-off-by: Anaël Closson <ac2550@intl.att.com>

Removed Urls not needed

Issue-ID: OPTFRA-22
Change-Id: I270de9cbed5f25dd971f40477fb95385a57fba5c
Signed-off-by: Ikram Ikramullah <ikram@research.att.com>

Removed Music IPs

MK:
PS-14: fixing robot testcase by forking the testcase until it is fixed in robot
       adding target port to osdf service.  required to map port 8698:8699

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

my limited review was ok

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(8 comments)

I've looked at all of the changesets on this particular topic (OOF add to OOM). Its really hard to tell which is the cardinal change. Updates should be kept within the changeset if they are on the same subject, attempting to do the same thing. See: https://www.mediawiki.org/wiki/Gerrit/Tutorial#Amending_a_change_(your_own_or_someone_else's) 

Keeping everything in the same changeset while you work through issues and changes makes reviewing and testing easier for others and it keeps a sane history for people to look back at if there are ever issues with the patch. In sum its a stellar idea to kept your changes on a specific subject together by pushing a new patch-set each time and not pushing a new changeset.

I've had a hard time pulling and testing locally as I'm trying to sort and find the latest patchset and test against SO.

Patch Set 1:

Also, shouldn't we have global config in oom/kubernetes/oof/values.yaml, so that a user doesn't have to edit each sub charts values.yaml?

Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1

Ankit - Why have you added changes in oof-has? You were suppose to do changes in oof-osdf folder. I have been doing these changes since morning and now I see all my files written over. 

You need to revert these changes you just pushed. Work in osdf only.

Patch Set 9:

Few more notes.

1. We are now only using three NodePorts ... 30275 (oof-has-api), 30275 (oof-has-music) and 30280 (oof-osdf).

2. The healthcheck API will now work off the bat - 

exec -it onap-oof-has-api-58c545cb95-d9kk8 -n onap -- curl http://onap-oof-has-api:8091/v1/plans/healthcheck

Will return our healthcheck sample response.

{"plans": [{"status": "done", "name": "foo", "links": [[{"href": "http://onap-oof-has-api:8091/v1/plans/healthcheck", "rel": "self"}]], "id": "healthcheck"}]}

Hope this will suffice for a merge with a +2 :)

Patch Set 13:

Well, it it looks like the changes are all coming due to the new changes you have made.

And yes, I donot see the healthcheck working anymore.

I see the cassandra namespace totally empty (created one temporary called oof3 to test your changes in it. See below, the data directory is totally empty.

nelson24@vm-conductor-04:~/oom.bak/oom-ikram6/oom/kubernetes$ ls -ltr /dockerdata-nfs/onap/oof3/cassandra/data/
total 0

Vs the older oof directly with my patch where the cassandra system and tables are properly setup.

nelson24@vm-conductor-04:~/oom.bak/oom-ikram6/oom/kubernetes$ ls -ltr /dockerdata-nfs/onap/oof/cassandra/data/
total 32
drwxr-xr-x 24 999 docker 4096 May  2 16:05 system
drwxr-xr-x 12 999 docker 4096 May  2 16:05 system_schema
drwxr-xr-x  4 999 docker 4096 May  2 16:05 system_traces
drwxr-xr-x  4 999 docker 4096 May  2 16:05 system_distributed
drwxr-xr-x  6 999 docker 4096 May  2 16:05 system_auth
drwxr-xr-x  3 999 docker 4096 May  2 16:05 admin
drwxr-xr-x  3 999 docker 4096 May  2 16:05 conductor
drwxr-xr-x  4 999 docker 4096 May  2 16:05 conductor_rpc


Cherry pick pacth 11 - change the direcotry in oof-has-cassandra/values.yaml to any good name of your choice, everything will work without any issue.

In sum, take a look at what you changed that caused the cassandra to not create our default tables anymore.

 > drwxr-xr-x 24 999 docker 4096 May  2 16:05 system

 > to any good name of your choice, everything will work without any
 > issue.

 > In sum, take a look at what you changed that caused the cassandra
 > to not create our default tables anymore.

Ikram,

I didn’t actually touch the Cassandra (or any of the has helm charts) in my changes.
I’m not sure what Cassandra namespace you are referring too. 
Here is the command I used to deploy OOF:

helm upgrade -i dev ./onap --namespace onap

In this example the release name is ‘dev’. The release name is what the Cassandra helm chart uses to create its persistent volume.

So in my environment the tables are created but under ‘dev’.

root@k8s-7:/dockerdata-nfs# ls -ltr /dockerdata-nfs/dev/oof/cassandra/data/
total 32
drwxr-xr-x 24 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:41 system
drwxr-xr-x 12 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:41 system_schema
drwxr-xr-x  4 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:41 system_traces
drwxr-xr-x  4 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:41 system_distributed
drwxr-xr-x  6 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:41 system_auth
drwxr-xr-x  3 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:42 admin
drwxr-xr-x  3 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:42 conductor
drwxr-xr-x  4 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:42 conductor_rpc

I redeployed using: helm upgrade -i onap ./onap --namespace onap instead and I got the same as you:

root@k8s-7:/dockerdata-nfs# ls -ltr /dockerdata-nfs/onap/oof/cassandra/data/
total 32
drwxr-xr-x 24 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:49 system
drwxr-xr-x 12 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:49 system_schema
drwxr-xr-x  4 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:49 system_traces
drwxr-xr-x  4 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:49 system_distributed
drwxr-xr-x  6 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:49 system_auth
drwxr-xr-x  3 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:50 admin
drwxr-xr-x  3 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:50 conductor
drwxr-xr-x  4 999 docker 4096 May  3 01:50 conductor_rpc

Unfortunately, health check still does not pass and the same errors exist in the containers.

Without my patch, containers are blocked and never come up b/c there are some incorrect service names and configuration.

How are you deploying and testing your helm charts?

If you do see a problem with the changes I made please by all means amend the patch to correct.

Thanks,
Mike.

Patch Set 14: Code-Review+2 Verified+1
